prs.title: fix: guard against null WhatsApp media downloads
prs.body:
## Summary
Fixes #440

## Changes Made
- Added validation after `msg.downloadMedia()`
- Check for missing media data or mimetype before building payload
- Log media download failures
- Send a specific retry message to the user when media processing fails
- Prevent null media from causing runtime errors

## Testing
- Verified media payload is only created when valid media exists
- Verified users receive a retry message when media download fails

prs.title: feat: add ease-flash rapid visibility animation utility (#590)

labels.name: animation
labels.description: Animation effects, hover interactions, motion ideas, transitions

labels.name: component
labels.description: New UI components (buttons, cards, modals, tooltips, badges)

labels.name: accepted
labels.description: Contribution approved for integration into EaseMotion CSS

labels.name: integrated
labels.description: Successfully merged and included in the framework

prs.body:
## Description

Implemented `ease-flash` rapid opacity animation utility for EaseMotion CSS.

### Features Added

- Rapid opacity toggle animation (`1 → 0 → 1 → 0 → 1`)
- Fast `0.8s` timing
- One-shot flash by default
- `--ease-flash-count` support
- Infinite flash variant available

### Demo & Documentation

Included:

- `demo.html`
- `style.css`
- `README.md`

### Enhancements

- Accessibility improvements
- `prefers-reduced-motion` support
- Responsive demo layout
- Production-ready utility styling
- Multiple real-world UI examples

Closes #590

prs.body:
## Description

Implemented `ease-spin` continuous rotation utility for EaseMotion CSS.

### Features Added

- Continuous `360°` infinite rotation
- Smooth linear timing
- `--ease-spin-speed` CSS variable support (default `1s`)
- `ease-spin-reverse` counter-rotation variant

### Demo & Documentation

Included:

- `demo.html`
- `style.css`
- `README.md`

### Enhancements

- Responsive demo layout
- Multiple usage examples
- Accessibility improvements
- Production-ready utility styling

Closes #581

Implemented the `ease-divider` component inside `submissions/examples/ease-divider/`.

### Features Added

✅ Simple divider with fade-in animation
✅ Labeled divider with centered content
✅ Gradient divider with fading edges
✅ Animated divider with center-draw effect
✅ Responsive and accessible implementation
✅ Demo showcase and documentation included

### Files Added

* demo.html
* style.css
* README.md

Closes #600

* Cleaned up `App.tsx` import structure for better consistency and maintainability

prs.title: feat: add interactive demo section to readme
prs.body: Added a "Try it now" section with StackBlitz/Replit demo links to make Lingo.dev more approachable and easy to test.

issues.title: Add Interactive Demo or Live Playground to Home Page
issues.body:
Feature Request: Add Interactive Demo or Live Playground to Home Page

Currently, the Lingo.dev repository README provides code samples and documentation links, but it does not have a clear, immediate interactive demo or preview feature on the home page.

Adding a live demo or playground section (for React, CLI, or SDK) would help new users quickly understand and test Lingo.dev capabilities without needing a full local setup. This improves onboarding, makes the project more approachable for first-time visitors, and showcases real project features instantly.

Suggestions:

Integrate a “Try it now” section or hosted playground/app in the README.

Link to demo apps showcasing main features (Compiler, CLI, CI/CD, SDK).

Optionally, embed interactive demo using StackBlitz, CodeSandbox, or similar platforms.

Thank you for considering this enhancement! It will make adoption easier for beginners and help demonstrate Lingo.dev’s value at a glance.
